metadata.title: Web Server Guide
metadata.description: Learn how to upload EPUB, XTC, and XTCH files wirelessly using the built-in web server in CrossPoint Reader.
metadata.ogDescription: Learn how to upload files wirelessly using the built-in web server in CrossPoint Reader.

hero.title: Web Server Guide
hero.subtitle: Learn how to upload and manage EPUB files over Wi-Fi.

overview.title: Overview
overview.intro: CrossPoint Reader includes a built-in web server that lets you:

features.upload: Upload EPUB files from any device on the same Wi-Fi network
features.browse: Browse and manage files on the SD card
features.folder: Create folders to organize your e-book library
features.download: Direct book download (v1.2.0): Download books from the SD card to your browser through the web UI
features.webdav: WebDAV support (v1.1+): Mount the device as a network drive in macOS Finder, Windows Explorer, Cyberduck, and similar clients

requirements.client: A computer, smartphone, or tablet connected to the same Wi-Fi network

items.settings: From the main menu or file browser, open the Settings screen
items.scan: The device automatically begins scanning for available networks

step2.title: Step 2: Connect to Wi-Fi
step2.availableNetworksIntro: When the scan finishes, the device displays available Wi-Fi networks with the following indicators:

indicators.lock: * symbol means the network is password-protected (encrypted)
indicators.saved: + symbol means saved credentials are available

selectNetwork.navigate: Use the Left/Right (or Prev/Down) buttons to scroll the network list
selectNetwork.confirm: Press OK to select the highlighted network

step2.passwordIntro: If the network requires a password:

passwordSteps.navigate: Use the navigation buttons to select characters
passwordSteps.done: When finished, choose the Done option on the keyboard

step2.note: If you have connected to this network before, the saved password is used automatically.

step3.title: Step 3: Connection Established
step3.important: Make a note of the IP address — you will need it to reach the web interface from a computer or smartphone.

computer.sameWifi: Verify your computer is on the same Wi-Fi network as the CrossPoint Reader
computer.openBrowser: Open a web browser (Chrome is recommended)
computer.enterIp: Type the IP address shown on the device into the address bar

mobile.sameWifi: Verify your phone or tablet is on the same Wi-Fi network as the CrossPoint Reader
mobile.openBrowser: Open a mobile browser (Safari, Chrome, etc.)
mobile.enterIp: Type the IP address into the address bar

step5.title: Step 5: Use the Web Interface
step5.fileManagerIntro: Click File Manager to access the file management features.

browse.show: All files and folders on the SD card are listed
browse.click: Click a folder name to enter that folder
browse.path: Use the breadcrumb path at the top to move to a parent folder

upload.addButton: Click the + Add button in the top right
upload.selectMenu: Choose Upload eBook from the dropdown menu
upload.chooseFile: Click Choose File and select an .epub file
upload.progress: A progress bar shows the upload status
upload.refresh: The page refreshes automatically when the upload completes

step5.uploadNote: Only .epub files are accepted. Other file types are rejected.

newFolder.addButton: Click the + Add button in the top right
newFolder.selectMenu: Choose New Folder from the dropdown menu
newFolder.name: Enter a folder name (letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens only)

delete.icon: Click the trash icon next to the file or folder
delete.confirm: Confirm the deletion in the popup dialog

step5.deleteWarning: Deletion is permanent and cannot be undone. Folders must be empty before they can be deleted.

notVisible.title: Device Not Reachable on the Network
notVisible.problem: The browser shows "Cannot connect" or "This site can't be reached".

items.sameWifi: Verify both devices are on the same Wi-Fi network
items.ip: Confirm the IP address was entered correctly
items.http: Include http:// at the start of the address

items.closer: Move closer to the Wi-Fi router
items.signal: Check the signal strength on the device (|| or higher recommended)

items.valid: Confirm the file is a valid .epub file
items.space: Verify the SD card has enough free space
items.smaller: Test with a smaller file first
items.refresh: Refresh the browser page and try again

items.port: The web server runs on port 80 (standard HTTP)
items.noAuth: No authentication is required — anyone on the same network can access the interface
items.connected: The web server is reachable only while the Wi-Fi screen shows "Connected"
items.stop: Leaving the Wi-Fi screen automatically stops the web server
items.trusted: For security, use only on trusted private networks

exit.title: Exit Wi-Fi Mode

steps.back: Press the Back button on the CrossPoint Reader

exit.success: Uploaded files are immediately available in the file browser!
